title: 3 patterns to build long-running AI agents
summary: Google Cloud Tech discusses three core architectural patterns for long-running AI agents: event-driven dormancy, durable checkpointing, and separated evaluation. These patterns enable agents to manage multi-day workflows beyond stateless chatbots.
writeup: The video presents three architectural patterns for building long-running AI agents: event-driven dormancy, durable checkpointing, and separated evaluation. These patterns address agents that must handle multi-day workflows rather than the stateless interactions typical of chatbots. Dormancy lets agents idle until triggered by events, checkpointing preserves state durably across long executions, and separated evaluation decouples assessment from the main agent loop. The discussion also touches on subagents as part of the agent design.
